title: Runtime Request Schemas, Negotiation, Problem Details, and OpenAPI · Guided Contract Studio
summary: A disability-led research network incrementally builds runtime-validated documented API boundary from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and browser fixtures while retaining prior TypeScript, Node, HTTP-client, Express, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, async-cleanup, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of runtime-validated documented API boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit th…
objectives: Validate params, query, headers, body, content type, size, cardinality, and unknown fields before constructing a command.
Define string, number, boolean, date, enum, array, empty, repeated, and default coercion explicitly and test ambiguity.
Map stable domain and infrastructure causes to bounded RFC 9457 types, statuses, field evidence, correlation, and retry guidance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Ts Request Unknown Admi…
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop TypeScript team handles case 6326 during a cross-tenant privacy and cache audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove runtime request admission, coercion, problem details, negotiation, and OpenAPI conformance; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate params, query, headers, body, content type, size, cardinality, and unknown fields before constructing a command.
